input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Spacing="16" Padding="20">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The binding automatically re-evaluates when the device theme changes — no event handling needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or setup method:
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 24 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#F0F0F0"));

// For non-color properties, use SetAppTheme<T>:
label.SetAppTheme<string>(Label.TextProperty, "Light Mode", "Dark Mode");
```

---

**Key points:**

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Trigger | Automatic on `Application.RequestedTheme` change |
| Scope | Per-property; no global toggle code needed |
| Resources | You can also theme via `AppThemeBinding` inside `ResourceDictionary` styles for reuse |

**Tip:** For larger apps, define themed colors in `Resources/Styles/Colors.xaml` and reference them, rather than inlining hex values everywhere.
